prompt: Develop a DiagramFlow Offline Canvas Studio enabling product managers, implementation consultants, and operations staff to create process flows, wireframes, and mind maps entirely offline in a zoomable, drag-and-drop canvas experience. The app runs as an Angular + TypeScript single-page application with an English UI and a left Sidebar for template selection, a central Canvas with draggable nodes and routed connection lines, and a right Drawer for node properties. Users access the tool via a local-only username + password pseudo-login stored on the device; after 3 incorrect password attempts the profile enters a 15-minute local cooldown, and after 8 hours of inactivity the app returns to the login screen (both are convenience behaviors and can be reset by clearing browser data). The canvas provides 20 built-in templates (process, mind map, and page layout families), snap-to-grid at 8 px with alignment guides, zoom from 25% to 400%, and Undo/Redo up to 200 steps with a History modal. Business limits are enforced with inline warnings and toasts: each canvas supports up to 500 nodes and 1,000 connections; node label text is limited to 200 characters and notes to 1,000 characters. For rapid population, users can import local JSON/CSV (up to 1,000 node rows per import) through a guided Import wizard with column mapping, row-level validation errors, and a reject/accept preview table. To support structured parsing and field standardization, the import and paste panel can also parse offline HTML/JSON snippets into configurable fields (e.g., role, salary in USD, location, experience, education, skills), normalize ranges/units, map synonyms, and standardize skill tags for consistent node metadata. Publishing and version control separates Draft canvases from Published snapshots via Tabs, enforces a pre-publish checklist (no dangling connectors, required title, within node/edge limits), shows side-by-side diffs of changes, and supports a configurable local approval toggle where a Reviewer must confirm publish/rollback. A Notification Center provides in-app messages only (no email/SMS), with templated variables (e.g., {canvasName}, {versionId}), a local send queue with retry-on-failure for UI delivery, and user do-not-disturb hours. All data persists locally with a tiered storage model: LocalStorage keeps lightweight preferences (theme, grid visibility, DND settings, last-opened canvas, and profile cooldown timestamps), while IndexedDB stores canvases, nodes/edges, template customizations, imported datasets, and version snapshots. Each open canvas auto-saves the most recent 30 versions, captured every 10 seconds while the app is open and changes are detected, and users can replay versions through a timeline scrubber and one-click rollback (with confirmation for destructive actions). Exports are user-initiated and offline: PNG and SVG are rendered from Canvas/SVG layers, and JSON exports include metadata and normalized tags; files are downloaded via Blob/FileSaver and may optionally use the File System Access API for Save As workflows. A Service Worker caches app assets for installable PWA-style offline use, Web Workers handle heavy exports/diff calculations to keep interactions responsive, and BroadcastChannel coordinates multi-tab edits by warning on concurrent changes and prompting the user to choose the latest local version.
